---
title: "LRPDID08: Waitlist-crossover (DiD) ITT effect on taught receptive vocabulary, block 1 (b1retau) (TR)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by an LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPDID08 is the **waitlist-crossover / difference-in-differences** analysis for
taught receptive vocabulary, block 1 (`b1retau`) - a within-person replication of the randomised ITT effect
estimated by LRPITT01. It stacks the two early periods (P1 = t1->t2, P2 = t2->t3)
for both arms; each child is their **own control** (a child random intercept), and
the immediate arm - treated in both periods - anchors the time/maturation trend.

::: {.callout-note title="What this estimand is, and is not"}

The waitlist arm is the **only untreated cell** (P1) and crosses to the
intervention in P2. The treatment coefficient is a **difference-in-differences**:
the waitlist's P1->P2 gain jump *over and above* the immediate arm's P1->P2 change.
It is identified jointly by the period-1 between-arm contrast and the waitlist's own
crossover, with stable child traits differenced out by the random intercept.

It is **not** a second randomised experiment: the P2 contrast is not randomised, so
identification leans on the parallel-trends assumption and the immediate arm as an
(imperfect, also-treated) time anchor. Read it as **triangulation** of the RCT, not
an independent replication; the two share the waitlist-P1 untreated cell.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Sign convention and the ceiling"}

Positive => the intervention helps. The Beta-Binomial **logit** link absorbs
ceiling effects: an immediate-arm child near the top of a bounded test makes a
small P2 gain *expected*, not a spurious negative time trend - which is exactly the
failure mode of a raw-gain DiD on the count scale.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_{i,p} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period}}\,[p = \text{P2}]
           + \delta\,\text{Treated}_{i,p}
           + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}_*(TR_{\mathrm{pre},i,p}, N)
           + \gamma_A\,A_{\text{std},i,p} + u_{\text{child},i},
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood on the period post-count, $N = 24$. ``Treated`` is
1 when the child is receiving the intervention that period (immediate: both;
waitlist: P2 only). Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_did.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
